title: Why Italians Saw the Pope Very Differently - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses historical perspectives on how Italians perceived papal authority and the papacy differently from other European populations. The episode explores cultural and religious contexts that shaped Italian attitudes toward the Pope during a particular historical period.
writeup: Historian Ada Palmer examines why Italians regarded papal authority and the papacy differently from other European populations during a particular historical period. The conversation traces the cultural and religious contexts that shaped distinctly Italian attitudes toward the Pope. The takeaway is that perceptions of papal authority were not uniform across Europe, and local context conditioned how the office was understood.
